text.title: A/B Testing Implementation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the ab-testing-implementation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every experiment event is a typed dataclass / Pydantic model with experiment_id, user_id, variant_id, kind in {exposure, conversion}, ts, properties.
rule.rationale: Untyped events drift; downstream warehouse loaders silently break on new keys.

rule.statement: ExperimentTracker deduplicates exposures within a request: the same (experiment_id, user_id) emits at most one exposure per request.
rule.rationale: Without dedup, a single request can fire 10 exposures and inflate denominators by 10×, fabricating significance.

rule.statement: Use Wilson score intervals for proportion CIs, not the naive normal approximation.
rule.rationale: Normal CIs are wrong near 0 and 1 (extend below 0 or above 1); Wilson handles edges correctly even at small n.

rule.statement: Reports show absolute and relative lift with 95% CI, not just the p-value.
rule.rationale: p-values without effect size invite p < 0.05 results that have no business impact.

rule.statement: Analyzer surfaces guardrail metrics (latency p95, error rate) alongside the primary metric; auto-flag >= 10% regression.
rule.rationale: Wins on primary metric that ship a 30% latency regression are not wins.

rule.statement: If you are on a managed platform (Optimizely, GrowthBook, Statsig) with a built-in analyzer, skip this methodology and use the platform.
rule.rationale: Reimplementing platform analytics introduces statistical bugs without business benefit.
